an1.10:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single touch that so completely occupies a woman's mind [*citta*] as this — the touch of a man.
an1.10:1.2: The touch of a man, *bhikkhus*, occupies a woman's mind [*citta*]."
an1.10:1.4: The first chapter, on Forms, Etc.
an1.1:1.2: at one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's Park.
an1.1:2.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single form that so completely occupies a man's mind [*citta*] as this — the form of a woman.
an1.1:2.2: The form of a woman, *bhikkhus*, occupies a man's mind [*citta*]."
an1.2:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single sound that so completely occupies a man's mind [*citta*] as this — the sound of a woman.
an1.2:1.2: The sound of a woman, *bhikkhus*, occupies a man's mind [*citta*]."
an1.3:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single scent that so completely occupies a man's mind [*citta*] as this — the scent of a woman.
an1.3:1.2: The scent of a woman, *bhikkhus*, occupies a man's mind [*citta*]."
an1.4:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single taste that so completely occupies a man's mind [*citta*] as this — the taste of a woman.
an1.4:1.2: The taste of a woman, *bhikkhus*, occupies a man's mind [*citta*]."

an1.6:1.2: The form of a man, *bhikkhus*, occupies a woman's mind [*citta*]."
